description: Bull, Base, and Bear case scenarios for NVIDIA with probability weights and key assumptions.

bull.name: Bull Case
bull.description: Data center market dominance sustained, AI TAM expands faster than expected, NVIDIA takes 87%+ market share through 2030. Margins remain at 74-75% as product mix improves. Blackwell and Rubin ramps exceed guidance.

key_drivers: Hyperscaler capex acceleration driven by LLM inference demand
CUDA ecosystem moat prevents major share loss to AMD/custom silicon
Blackwell Ultra and Rubin architectures deliver 2-3x performance gains
China export controls plateau at current levels, no escalation
AI infrastructure TAM grows to $4.5T+ by 2030

key_risks: AMD catches up faster with MI400 series

base.name: Base Case
base.description: NVIDIA maintains strong position with market share moderation to 80% as AMD gains ground and custom silicon ramps. Growth moderates from double digits to high single digits by Year 5. Gross margins compress gradually to 71% due to mix shift toward inference.

key_drivers: Data center TAM grows 15-18% annually through 2030
AMD gains 10-15% market share but CUDA lock-in persists
Mix shift from training (75% margin) to inference (65% margin) compresses overall GM
China export controls remain in place but don't materially worsen
Rubin and Vera Rubin maintain competitive edge but not as pronounced as Blackwell

bear.name: Bear Case
bear.description: Competitive dynamics intensify sharply. AMD and custom silicon capture 25-30% of market. Growth slows to 5-8% as TAM plateau begins. Margins compress to 64% as pricing power erodes. Hypersecaler capex cycle softens.

key_drivers: AMD MI400/MI500 series takes 20%+ market share by 2027-28
Google TPU 8, Amazon Trainium, and custom silicon mature rapidly
Hyperscaler capex growth slows from 35%+ CAGR to 8-10% as AI ROI questioned
China export controls escalate; lose access to 15-20% of market
Blackwell transition creates 6-12 month disruption in shipments
Geopolitical fragmentation creates regional competition (China, EU)

key_risks: Structural market share loss to competitors
Margin compression from pricing power loss

sources: NVIDIA Q4 FY2026 earnings (Feb 2026 press release)
Company backlog disclosure ($500B cumulative through calendar 2026)
Industry TAM estimates ($3-4T AI infrastructure by 2030)

notes.current_price_not_revealed: Director reveals stock price in Pass 2 after analyst fair values submitted
data_gaps: Exact customer concentration percentages not disclosed
Operating expense detail (R&D vs SG&A) estimated
Free cash flow conversion estimated from gross margin and minimal capex
key_sensitivities: Terminal gross margin: ±300 bps changes valuation by ~30%
Market share assumption: each 1% share loss = ~3-5% valuation impact
China export control impact: estimates +/- $5-10B annual revenue variance
